results.clinical_note: 61yo european male newly diagnosed autoimmune hepatitis – liver edge felt just below RCM. Screen for HCC, cirrhosis

ai_response.suggested_wording: 61yo European male newly diagnosed autoimmune hepatitis with hepatomegaly (liver edge palpable just below right costal margin). Non-pulsatile abdominal mass and suspected enlarged liver on examination. Request ultrasound abdomen to assess liver size and morphology.
ai_response.redirect: Consider referral to gastroenterology for autoimmune hepatitis management and HCC surveillance planning
ai_response.notes: The referral mentions HCC screening but the S3 pathway for HCC surveillance requires initial recommendation by gastroenterologist. However, the clinical finding of hepatomegaly meets P3 criteria independently. The note could be reworded to focus on the hepatomegaly assessment rather than HCC surveillance to meet criteria.

Note: Given the location uncertainty (?knee ?ankle), consider also imaging the ankle if knee X-ray is normal and symptoms persist.

results.clinical_note: 75yo m w/ new AKI, eGFR 3 (normally 55). No clear cause.
results.evaluator_verdict: Proceeds — surface P1 AKI and P2, note conflicting dispositions

ai_response.verdict_summary: Meets criteria for acute renal function deterioration with dramatic eGFR drop requiring urgent imaging.
met_criteria: Acute renal function deterioration — serum creatinine increase >50 micromol/L in 7 days or >25 micromol/L in 2 days [p53]
add_to_note: Document the timeframe over which this eGFR decline occurred (7 days or 2 days) to confirm acute deterioration criteria
Consider documenting creatinine values in micromol/L if available
ai_response.suggested_wording: 75-year-old male with acute kidney injury. eGFR dropped from baseline 55 to current 3 over [specify timeframe]. No clear precipitating cause identified. Request urgent renal ultrasound to exclude obstruction or structural abnormality.
ai_response.notes: The dramatic eGFR drop from 55 to 3 represents severe acute kidney injury. While the criteria specify creatinine increases in micromol/L, an eGFR drop of this magnitude (>50 point decline) clearly meets the threshold for acute deterioration. The note recommends discussion with secondary care/PCRL which would be appropriate given the severity.

results.clinical_note: 3 months lower back pain after stepping down from a ladder. no radiation, no bowel symptoms. no fevers. not tender to palpation. normal lower limb exam.

ai_response.notes: The presentation clearly describes a trauma mechanism (stepping down from a ladder) as the cause of the back pain. Per CRR criteria, history of recent trauma should be funded via ACC, not CRR.

missing_criteria: MHT type (continuous combined vs other) and duration since starting therapy [p46]

ai_response.suggested_wording: 56 year old post menopausal woman on [specify type] hormone replacement therapy for [duration] with 2 days of PV bleeding
ai_response.notes: Two potential pathways identified: (1) General post-menopausal bleeding pathway is fully met and would proceed as P2 Urgent. (2) MHT-specific pathway requires additional details about MHT type and duration. The general pathway alone is sufficient for acceptance, but MHT details would help determine the most appropriate pathway.

However, explicit documentation of string visibility would strengthen the referral.
